title: One-command release-version bump: shared tuple registry + bump_version CLI + de-hardcoded split-brain anchors
action: Extract the release-version tuple location registry (COMPONENTS + read/write) into side-effect-free scripts/lib/release-tuple.mjs; refactor test_release_tuple.mjs to import it (check side); add scripts/bump_version.mjs (write side) that sets every tuple member from one command + regenerates the manifest; add scripts/test_bump_version.mjs; de-hardcode the current-version anchors in test_split_brain_regression.mjs to derive from templates/lib/state.mjs; wire test_bump_version into commands.verify; spec sync.

truths: bump_version.mjs writes all 4 tuple components from one command and post-verifies they agree
the split-brain regression fixture derives its current-version expectation from templates/lib/state.mjs, no hardcoded literal
test_bump_version proves the writer covers every registry component and preserves each file's surrounding bytes

trace.verification_evidence: Release-version single-source: extracted the tuple-location registry to scripts/lib/release-tuple.mjs; test_release_tuple imports it (check), new bump_version.mjs writes every member + regenerates manifest (write). De-hardcoded split-brain anchors to derive current version from templates/lib/state.mjs. Added test_bump_version (covers all registry components, preserves surrounding bytes). Wired into verify. Full suite green (614 PASS, exit 0). Round-trip bump 1.3.5->1.3.6->1.3.5 leaves the 4 tuple files byte-identical (empty git diff).
trace.verify_output:
PASS test_bump_version: CLI rejects an unknown flag
PASS test_bump_version: CLI --check exits 0 on the real in-sync tuple and writes nothing
